description: Implement tile randomization for Favor of the Pharaoh. For later games per the rules, randomly deal 1 tile of each matching color per bar slot instead of the fixed first-game setup. Builds pure C# in src/FotP.Engine/Market/. Tile pool: 27 yellow types, 12 blue types, 15 red types (54 total) across levels 3-7. Physical tile counts (63Y/36B/36R = 135) derive from stacking (players-1) copies per slot. Dependency graph: WP1+WP2 parallel → WP3 → WP4 → WP5.

nodes.title: 01-tile-definition-and-catalog
nodes.description:
Create the tile type data layer. Two files in src/FotP.Engine/Market/.

## TileDefinition.cs
Immutable record representing a tile type (not a runtime Tile instance):
```csharp
namespace FotP.Engine.Market;
public record TileDefinition(string Id, string Name, int Level, TileColor Color);
```
The Id is a kebab-case unique key (e.g. "farmer", "spirit-of-dead"). Abilities are NOT included here — they'll be wired when the ability system is built.

## TileCatalog.cs
Static class registering all 54 tile types from the rules (docs/fotp-rules-v20-plan.yaml, tile-catalog-level3 through level7):

### Level 3 (11 types)
- Yellow (7): Farmer, Guard, Indentured Worker, Serf, Worker, Beggar, Servant
- Blue (1): Soothsayer
- Red (3): Ankh, Ancestral Guidance, Omen

### Level 4 (11 types)
- Yellow (6): Artisan, Builder, Noble Adoption, Palace Servants, Soldier, Grain Merchant
- Blue (2): Entertainer, Match Maker
- Red (3): Good Omen, Palace Key, Spirit of the Dead

### Level 5 (11 types)
- Yellow (5): Charioteer, Conspirator, Overseer, Ship Captain, Master Artisan
- Blue (3): Tomb Builder, Head Servant, Priest
- Red (3): Bad Omen, Burial Mask, Royal Decree

### Level 6 (11 types)
- Yellow (4): Embalmer, Estate Overseer, Royal Attendants, Priestess
- Blue (4): Grain Trader, Priest of the Dead, Astrologer, Surveyor
- Red (3): Pharaoh's Gift, Secret Passage, Treasure

### Level 7 (10 types)
- Yellow (5): Queen, General, Grand Vizier, Granary Master, Heir
- Blue (2): Royal Astrologer, Royal Mother
- Red (3): Queen's Favor, Royal Death, Royal Power

### API
```csharp
public static class TileCatalog
{
    public static IReadOnlyList<TileDefinition> All { get; }
    public static IReadOnlyList<TileDefinition> ByLevel(int level);
    public static IReadOnlyList<TileDefinition> ByColor(TileColor color);
    public static IReadOnlyList<TileDefinition> ByColorAndLevel(TileColor color, int level);
    public static TileDefinition GetById(string id);
}
```
Internally backed by a static list + dictionary lookup, built once in a static constructor.

### Counts to verify
- Total: 54 types
- Yellow: 27 (7+6+5+4+5)
- Blue: 12 (1+2+3+4+2)
- Red: 15 (3+3+3+3+3)
- Per level: 11, 11, 11, 11, 10

nodes.title: 02-market-model
03-tile-pool
04-tile-dealer
05-unit-tests
nodes.description:
Comprehensive xUnit tests for all tile randomization components. Files in src/FotP.Engine.Tests/Market/.

Ensure FotP.Engine.Tests.csproj references xUnit packages and the FotP.Engine project.

## TileCatalogTests.cs
- `CatalogHas54TileTypes()` — TileCatalog.All.Count == 54
- `YellowCount_Is27()` — ByColor(Yellow).Count == 27
- `BlueCount_Is12()` — ByColor(Blue).Count == 12
- `RedCount_Is15()` — ByColor(Red).Count == 15
- `LevelCounts_AreCorrect()` — levels 3-6 have 11 each, level 7 has 10
- `AllIdsAreUnique()` — no duplicate IDs
- `GetById_ReturnsCorrectTile()` — spot-check a few tiles
- `GetById_ThrowsForUnknownId()`
- `ByColorAndLevel_ReturnsCorrectSubset()` — e.g. Yellow+L3 = 7 tiles

## TilePoolTests.cs
- `DrawRandom_ReturnsTileOfCorrectColorAndLevel()`
- `DrawRandom_ReducesRemainingCount()`
- `DrawRandom_ThrowsWhenExhausted()` — draw all L3 blue (1 tile), then draw again
- `DrawSpecific_ReturnsTileAndRemovesFromPool()`
- `DrawSpecific_ThrowsForMissingId()`
- `DrawRandom_WithSeed_IsDeterministic()` — same seed produces same draw order
- `TotalRemaining_DecreasesAfterDraw()`
- `CustomPoolConstructor_WorksWithSubset()`

## TileDealerTests.cs
- `Deal_FixedSetup_PlacesCorrectTiles()` — provide 3 BarSlotRequests with FixedTileIds, verify market has those tiles
- `Deal_RandomSetup_FillsAllSlots()` — 5 random requests, all slots non-null
- `Deal_RandomSetup_NoDuplicateTileTypes()` — deal all L3 yellow slots (up to 7), all different tile types
- `Deal_RandomSetup_IsDeterministic()` — same seed → same tiles
- `Deal_ThrowsForInvalidPlayerCount()` — playerCount 0, 1, 5
- `Deal_StackSize_EqualsPlayersMinusOne()` — 4 players → 3 tiles per stack
- `Validate_ReturnsEmpty_ForValidSetup()`
- `Validate_DetectsDuplicateFixedTiles()`
- `Validate_DetectsPoolExhaustion()` — request 8 L3 yellow random but only 7 exist

## MarketSlotTests.cs
- `ClaimTile_ReturnsTopTile()`
- `ClaimTile_ReducesStackCount()`
- `ClaimTile_ReturnsNull_WhenExhausted()`
- `IsExhausted_TrueAfterAllClaimed()`
- `Constructor_CreatesCorrectStackSize()`

## MarketTests.cs
- `GetSlotsByLevel_FiltersCorrectly()`
- `GetSlotsByColor_FiltersCorrectly()`
- `GetAvailableSlots_ExcludesExhausted()`
- `FindSlotByTileId_ReturnsCorrectSlot()`
- `FindSlotByTileId_ReturnsNull_ForMissingTile()`

### Test project setup
Add to FotP.Engine.Tests.csproj:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.*" />
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.*" />
<ProjectReference Include="..\FotP.Engine\FotP.Engine.csproj" />
```
